summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the DeregCallbackUri parameter is used without URL validation, allowing UDM to make HTTP requests to internal services.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause UDM to send an HTTP POST request to the attacker-controlled callback URL, demonstrating SSRF to internal network services.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the Docker image, started UDM and internal mock service, registered AMF with DeregCallbackUri=http://internal-service:8080/internal, and the internal service received POST request from UDM.
verification_method: The verify.sh script checks the internal service logs for the POST request received from UDM, confirming SSRF attack success.
evidence_produced_by_run: Internal mock service started and listening on port 8080
AMF registration with malicious DeregCallbackUri succeeded
Internal service logs show POST /internal from UDM container
SSRF attack confirmed: UDM sent request to internal-only service

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the free5gc/udm repository from GitHub, checks out v3.2.10 tag, and mounts that checkout into the container.
